answerLength.medium: 1-2 concise paragraphs when the prompt asks for examples.
answerLength.long: Use only when the employer asks for a detailed narrative.

preferredFraming: Lead with the direct answer first.
Use real examples from analytics, data engineering, teaching, or stakeholder work.
Connect technical work to business decisions or learner outcomes.
Stay honest about experience level and avoid overstating ownership.
For employer forms, use concise resume-style bullets rather than long narrative paragraphs.
When describing experience, lead with the business or operational context, then tools, then outcome.

preferredWording: Supported analytics, chatbot development, and technical education initiatives for students and faculty.
Mentored students on applied analytics, reporting, and business technology projects.
Delivered instruction in Python programming, web development, and practical problem-solving methodologies.
Assisted with project coordination, stakeholder communication, and educational technology implementation.
Built end-to-end analytical workflows using Python, SQL, Excel, and visualization tools to analyze operational and business datasets.
Conducted exploratory data analysis and trend identification to support data-driven recommendations and strategic insights.
Developed dashboards and KPI reporting tools in Tableau and Power BI for non-technical stakeholders.
Performed forecasting and predictive analytics projects involving customer behavior, operational performance, and financial indicators.
Collaborated on business-focused AI and analytics initiatives while managing multiple deliverables and deadlines.
Supported transportation systems operations by maintaining data pipelines and monitoring real-time system performance across 100+ sensor inputs.
Analyzed operational data and resolved system issues using SQL, Python, and Linux-based tools.
Assisted cross-functional technical teams in identifying process improvements and increasing system reliability.
Conducted data validation, troubleshooting, and reporting to support operational continuity and decision-making.
Coordinated issue resolution activities while ensuring timely completion of assigned operational projects.
Analyzed educational and research datasets using Python, SQL, Excel, and statistical methods to support university research initiatives.
Improved data processing and reporting efficiency by 30% through structured data cleaning and validation procedures.
Conducted trend analysis, quality assurance checks, and reporting to support strategic research objectives.
Prepared presentations and analytical summaries for faculty and research stakeholders.
Collaborated with interdisciplinary teams to support data-driven project initiatives and organizational goals.

standardApplicationResponses.howDidYouHearAboutUs: Use the employer career site when applying directly through the company website.
standardApplicationResponses.previouslyEmployedByEmployer: No unless the employer-specific answer changes.
standardApplicationResponses.seniorManagementRelativeOrCoveredRelatio: No unless the employer-specific answer changes.
standardApplicationResponses.recentKpmgEmployment: No unless the employer-specific answer changes.
standardApplicationResponses.seniorGovernmentOfficialRelationship: No unless the employer-specific answer changes.
standardApplicationResponses.seniorCommercialPersonReferral: No unless the employer-specific answer changes.
standardApplicationResponses.termsAndConditions: Review the employer statement, then answer yes only after reading and consenting.

formFillingRules: Treat compliance, work authorization, sponsorship, relationship, veteran, disability, demographic, and consent questions as user-confirmed only.
Do not infer or auto-change protected-class, disability, veteran, or demographic responses.
Do not store address, phone, government IDs, protected demographic responses, disability status, or other sensitive one-off form fields in generated packet files.
For standard yes/no questions, preserve the exact concise answer unless the user updates the baseline.
For experience descriptions, reuse the user's bullet structure and practical wording.

resumeFormatProfile.source: Resume_citi.docx provided 2026-06-06; American Airlines analyst packet provided 2026-07-11
layout: No decorative cards, icons, columns, ratings, or graphics.
Use concise section spacing rather than large visual gaps.
resumeFormatProfile.headlinePattern: Use a compact target headline such as ANALYST | BUSINESS ANALYTICS | DATA REPORTING for analyst/reporting roles, BUSINESS ANALYTICS | DATA ANALYSIS for business analyst roles, DATA ENGINEERING | ANALYTICS for data engineering roles, or DATA SCIENCE | ANALYTICS for data science roles.
resumeFormatProfile.summaryPattern: Use a short third-person professional summary that states focus area, core tools, reporting/operations value, and stakeholder communication. Avoid first-person language in the resume summary.
resumeFormatProfile.coreCompetenciesPattern: Use short standalone skill terms. Prioritize job-matched skills first.
experiencePattern: Four to five direct accomplishment/responsibility bullets per role.
Start bullets with action verbs such as Supported, Built, Conducted, Developed, Performed, Collaborated, Analyzed, Improved, Prepared, Coordinated.
Mention tools inside the business context rather than as isolated keyword stuffing.
Prefer operational, stakeholder, reporting, dashboard, validation, forecasting, and data-quality framing for analyst roles.
skillsPattern: Business Tools: tools and communication outputs
tailoringRules: Keep the resume plain, scannable, and ATS-friendly.
Reorder and emphasize existing evidence rather than adding unsupported claims.
Use the user's bullet language as the default voice for tailored resumes.
For each role, prioritize the most relevant four to five bullets instead of overloading the resume.
For banking, analytics, and data roles, emphasize SQL, Python, dashboards, data validation, KPI reporting, stakeholder communication, and operational decision support.
americanAirlinesAnalystCalibration: For American Airlines, transportation, operations, benefits, performance, reporting, and contact-center analytics roles, use the Analyst | Business Analytics | Data Reporting resume direction.
Emphasize performance reporting, KPI development, dashboard creation, process improvement, data validation, operational analysis, and communication with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Use Thales transportation systems experience as a strong bridge to airline operations, regulated environments, data accuracy, reliability, and cross-functional work.
Use a warm, grounded cover-letter tone: excited to apply, graduate-level analytics education, practical SQL/Python/Excel/Tableau/Power BI work, and interest in improving reporting processes.

avoid: Claims that go beyond the resume/profile evidence
Saving protected demographic or disability disclosures in application packet files
Treating voluntary disclosure answers as reusable writing examples
calibrationStatus: Calibrated from Citi employer form answers and Resume_citi.docx provided on 2026-06-06, then updated from the American Airlines analyst application packet provided on 2026-07-11. Sensitive personal, demographic, disability, and address fields were intentionally excluded.
